message:
perf(session): increase BufReader capacity to 1MB for JSONL session loading

Session loading in open_jsonl_with_diagnostics() performs a sequential
line-by-line scan of potentially large JSONL files (sessions with
hundreds of messages can reach tens of megabytes). The default BufReader
capacity of 8KB results in excessive read() syscalls — for a 10MB
session file, that's ~1,250 syscalls versus ~10 with a 1MB buffer.

This commit replaces `BufReader::new(file)` with
`BufReader::with_capacity(1 << 20, file)` (1MB buffer). Since session
loading is inherently sequential and the buffer is short-lived (dropped
after parsing), the extra 1MB allocation is negligible and immediately
reclaimed.

This reduces wall-clock time for loading large sessions, particularly
noticeable on --continue invocations with long conversation histories.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
